observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:


   a. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal  Treasurer  reconcile  their
      cash records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts
      per GL and cashbooks;


   b. Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) continue  to  make  representation  and
      follow-up with the  depository bank and request copies of  the credit
      and   debit   memoranda   totaling   ₱128,850.75   and    ₱528,795.95,
      respectively, and provide a copy of which to the Municipal  Accountant
      for proper adjustment, if warranted; (ii)  cause  the  refund  of  the
      ₱2,000.00 charged against the General  Fund  LBP-  Pinamalayan  Branch
      account for falling below the required ADB;  (iii)  regularly  monitor
      the bank balances of all  the  accounts  of  the  Municipality  if  it
      complies with the requirements of the  bank  to  avoid  incurrence  of
      additional bank charges; and (iv) coordinate  with the banks  for  the
      funds maintained in the bank  accounts  tagged  as  dormant  and  make
      appropriate actions relative thereto; and


   c. Municipal Accountant (i) conduct detailed review and checking of  bank
      records/reports and request for the necessary documents to support the
      recording of the reconciling items,  if  any;  (ii)  exert  continuous
      effort to substantiate the identified reconciling items and effect the
      necessary adjustments; (iii) submit the lacking  supporting  documents
      to substantiate the adjustment of bank reconciling items in GF  –  LBP
      Pinamalayan and TF – LBP Pinamalayan amounting  to  ₱120,338.86;   and
      (iv) review  the  long  outstanding/stale  checks  specifically  those
      checks without identified payees  and  require  the  Acting  Municipal
      Treasurer to  cancel  the  unclaimed  stale  checks  and  furnish  the
      Accountant with the original DVs and its supporting documents for  the
      preparation of the JEV following the procedures pursuant to the Manual
      on the NGAS for LGUs to ensure recording of such cancellation.

We recommended that the:

 a. Municipal Accountant adopt accrual basis of accounting and institute  a
    system of control relative to the recording of  collectibles  including
    surcharges  from  Public  Market  stallholders  and  Waterworks  System
    customers; and
 b. Municipal Accountant  and  Acting  Municipal  Treasurer  reconcile  the
    receivable balances from the operations of Public Market and Waterworks
    System, and  effect  the  necessary  adjustments  in  their  respective
    records, if warranted.

We recommended that the:


   a. Municipal Accountant  reconcile  with  the  Municipal  Agriculturist’s
      record regarding the negative balances of Other Receivables  amounting
      to ₱120,125.00 and make appropriate adjustments, if warranted; and


   b. Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal  Treasurer,  Accountant,  MSWDO
      and Agriculturist to exert continuous effort  in  retrieving  relevant
      documents to support the long outstanding receivables  recorded  under
      the Other Receivables account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

   a. Municipal Accountant (i) record the  supplies  and  materials  to  the
      Inventory account upon purchase  and  Expense  account  upon  issuance
      thereof pursuant to the  asset  method  of  accounting  for  recording
      purchases of supplies and materials as prescribed under Section 114 of
      the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs;


   b. GSO-Designate conduct physical count of Inventories  and  prepare  the
      RPCI per account in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual  on  the
      NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

   c. Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate  maintain  supplies  ledger
      card and supplies stock card with complete details for each  stock  of
      inventory to account the issuance of its inventories to avoid possible
      misuse and wastage, and conduct  regular  reconciliation  between  the
      Accounting and GSO records  for  inventory  accounts  and  effect  the
      necessary adjustments, if warranted; and

   d. Municipal Accountant and concerned offices (i)  provide  justification
      on the deficiencies in the distribution and balance  of  inventory  at
      year-end; (ii) submit to the Audit Team the lacking distribution lists
      pertaining to the procured  items,  if  any;  (iii)  conduct  complete
      accounting and reconcile the noted difference  in  the  quantities  of
      procured and distributed livelihood items; and (iv) ensure that  items
      procured and distributed are properly accounted and tallied  with  its
      respective records.

We recommended that the:

   a. Municipal  Mayor  consider  the  one-time  cleansing  of  PPE  account
      balances as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31,
      2020 to come up with a reliable PPE balance;


   b.  Inventory  Committee  (i)  conduct  a  complete  physical  count  and
      technical valuation of the property, plant and equipment to  establish
      the existence, ownership and condition of the properties owned by  the
      Municipality especially for the local road network  to  determine  the
      current value of the road components and facilitate the preparation of
      the RPCLRN; and (ii) ensure that the required details in  the  reports
      were accurately and completely filled out;


   c. Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate allot time and  effort  to  (i)
      coordinate and prepare complete supporting schedules to facilitate the
      reconciliation of the property accounts especially the previous year’s
      PPE acquisitions; (ii) prepare and maintain appropriate  and  required
      property records and reports for all PPE accounts especially  for  the
      Road Networks; and (iii) coordinate with  the  Municipal  Engineer  to
      complete the maintenance of a road map with complete  description  and
      cost segregation of road components for road projects;


   d. GSO-Designate (i) analyze the items included in the RPCPPE to  exclude
      non-PPE accounts such as expendable  and  semi-expendable  properties,
      and coordinate with  the  MAccO  for  the  proper  classification  and
      account name of items included in the RPCPPE;  and  (ii)  prepare  and
      maintain RLRN and  Road  Map  to  account  for  the  Local  Roads  and
      reconcile with the Registry of Public  Infrastructures  maintained  by
      the Accounting Office;


   e. Appraisal and Disposal Committee account all determined  unserviceable
      properties  to  facilitate  the  preparation  of  required  supporting
      documents for disposal;


   f. Municipal Accountant  ensure  proper  classification  of  accounts  in
      accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs  as  prescribed
      by COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015  and  review  the
      entries made  relevant  to  the  above  transactions  and  effect  the
      necessary adjustments, if warranted, and be guided by COA Circular No.
      2015-009 in  recording  similar  transactions  in  the  books  of  the
      Municipality; and


   g. Municipal Engineer maintain a  road  map  and  provide  the  Municipal
      Accountant and the GSO with complete description and cost  segregation
      of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the:


   a. Municipal Treasurer and other concerned offices  coordinate  with  the
      suppliers and employees to inform  them  of  the  unclaimed  payables,
      otherwise provide certification to  the  Municipal  Accounting  Office
      that there are no valid claimants for the long outstanding payables to
      facilitate the reversion of funds; and


   b. Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that only  obligations  with  approved
      disbursement vouchers and complete supporting documents with  delivery
      of goods or services rendered are booked as accounts payable  pursuant
      to the prevailing rules and regulations; (ii) review the entries  made
      on the misclassified payables and  effect  the  necessary  adjustments
      pursuant to Annex B of COA Circular No.  2015-009  dated  December  1,
      2015; and (iii) analyze and conduct a thorough review of the  Accounts
      Payable and  Other  Payables  under  the  GF  and  SEF  with  payables
      outstanding for more than two years to facilitate the reversion to the
      unappropriated surplus as mandated in Section 98 of PD No.  1445,  and
      effect the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  (i)  conduct  continuous
   review and  analysis  of  the  Due  to  LGUs  accounts  under  the  TF,  and
   reclassify the amount that were not sourced from the LGUs to  Due  to  GOCCs
   and Due to NGAs accounts pursuant to Annex B of COA  Circular  No.  2015-009
   dated December 1, 2015 prescribing the Revised Chart  of  Accounts  for  the
   LGUs, if warranted; and (ii) exert continuous effort to collate and  analyze
   the details about the long outstanding Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs  accounts
   to determine the necessary actions  regarding  these  unutilized  government
   funds.

We recommended that the:


   a. Municipal Accountant observe the proper recognition of  loan  payables
      for the fair presentation of the balances in the financial  statements
      of the Municipality; and


   b. Acting Municipal Treasurer secure and provide immediately the copy  of
      the credit memoranda and/or snapshot from the bank  to  the  Municipal
      Accountant to record the receipt of loan proceeds as well  as  payment
      of the loan amortization on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that  income  from
   grants received by the Municipality and disbursements relative  thereto  are
   properly recognized and  classified  in  the  books  of  accounts  for  fair
   presentation, transparency and accountability of the funds utilized.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant  review
the  entries  made  under  the  OMOE  account  and  effect   the   necessary
adjustments, if warranted and ensure that expenditures of  the  Municipality
are  properly  classified  for  its  fair  presentation  in  the   financial
statement in accordance with the Revised Chart  of  Accounts  prescribed  in
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.  Moreover,  ensure  proper
monitoring of charges under the account which should include maintenance  of
complete details and records of the transactions.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a)  install  sound
internal control on the collection and remittance by requiring  the Revenue
Collectors to remit their collections daily and avoid keeping  large  amount
of money in their possession; (b) ensure that all  collectors  are  properly
bonded  and  their  collections   are   made   within   the   maximum   cash
accountability allowed in their fidelity bond, and if  possible,  coordinate
with the Bureau of  Treasury  to  the  increase  their  respective  fidelity
bonds, if warranted; and (c) require the collectors to  properly  accomplish
the Report of Collections and Remittances/Deposits as to report  date,  date
of collection, mode of payment and signatures of  the  concerned  personnel,
among others.

We recommended that the:

   a. Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer  strictly  monitor
      accountable officers with outstanding cash advances and  refrain  from
      granting additional cash advances to an employee prior  to  settlement
      of his/her previous cash advances, and be guided in the proper use  of
      accounts relating to cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No.  2015-
      009; and

   b. Municipal Treasurer ensure that the  liquidation  of  the  accountable
      officers is within the reglementary  period  in  compliance  with  the
      prescribed guidelines under Sections 5.1.1 and 5.8 of COA Circular No.
      97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) facilitate the return
of the excess amount  from  the  fully  completed  projects  to  the  source
agencies pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013  dated  December
13, 1994; and (ii) continuously coordinate  with  the  implementing  offices
relative to the long outstanding fund transfers so that appropriate  actions
may be undertaken thereon for the effective  and  efficient  utilization  of
the funds.

We recommended that the:

   a. MHO provide justification on the deficiencies noted in the liquidation
      of COVID-19 Benefit grant and ensure the completeness and veracity  of
      documents before payments are made;

   b. Municipal Accountant ensure that payments  for  AHDP,  SRA  and  other
      allowances were supported with complete documentation; and

   c.  Concerned  Municipal  Officials  (i)  provide  justification  on  the
      charging of ineligible expenses under the 20% DF; (ii) comment on  the
      observations noted  on  the  procurement  process  undertaken  by  the
      Municipality during the  COVID-19  situation;  and  (iii)  submit  the
      lacking documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the:

   a. BAC Chairperson, thru the BAC Technical Working Group and Secretariat,
      provide explanation on the noted lapses specifically on the delays and
      deficiencies in the procurement process, inconsistencies in the  dates
      indicated  in  various  documents  and  the   absence   of   necessary
      procurement documents;


   b. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant provide explanation on the
      unsupported work suspension for the projects as mentioned and  delayed
      payments to the suppliers/contractors;

   c. BAC strictly observe the period of action provided in RA 9184 and  its
      RIRR within which certain stages of the procurement  process  must  be
      completed  and  adopt  the  latest  procurement  process   and   forms
      prescribed by the GPPB in conformity with the requirements of  Section
      6 of the RIRR on standardization of government procurement;

   d. MDRRMO and MSWDO (i) provide justification on the  deficiencies  noted
      in the distribution of food commodities and  housing  materials;  (ii)
      submit to the Audit Team the lacking distribution lists pertaining  to
      the procured welfare goods, if any; (iii) effect  complete  accounting
      for and reconcile the noted difference in the quantities  of  procured
      and distributed relief items;  and  (iv)  ensure  that  welfare  goods
      distributed are properly accounted and tallied with the record of  its
      procurement; and

   e.  Municipal  Accountant,  BAC,  and  the  Municipal  Engineer   provide
      explanation on the deficiencies observed in the  supporting  documents
      and submit the lacking documents.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   concerned
officials and employees to submit to the Audit Team  the  lacking  documents
for audit purposes, and Municipal Accountant ensure payments  for  RATA  and
other allowances were supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the:

   a. BAC Chairperson, thru the BAC Technical Working Group and Secretariat,
      provide explanation on the noted lapses specifically on the delays  in
      the procurement process, inconsistencies in  the  dates  indicated  in
      various documents and the absence of necessary procurement documents;


   b. BAC strictly observe the period of action provided in RA 9184 and  its
      RIRR within which certain stages of the procurement  process  must  be
      completed  and  adopt  the  latest  procurement  process   and   forms
      prescribed by the GPPB in conformity with the requirements of  Section
      6 of the RIRR on standardization of government procurement; and


   c. Municipal Accountant, BAC, and  the  Municipal  Engineer  explain  the
      deficiencies observed in the  supporting  documents,  and  submit  the
      lacking documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the:

 a. Municipal Engineer (i) monitor and  require  the  contractors  to  make
    corrective actions on the defects noted in the Concreting of Local Road
    at Barangay B. Del Mundo, Construction of  Local  Road,  Brgy.  B.  del
    Mundo, and 500 LM Local Access Road at Barangay Maliwanag  and  apprise
    the Audit Team on the actions taken thereto, (ii)  submit  the  lacking
    documents for the nine projects for further review  and  evaluation  of
    the COA TAS; and (iii) ensure compliance with COA Resolution No.  2015-
    014 dated April 6,  2015 and DPWH DO No. 197 dated October 7,  2016  and
    other applicable laws, rules and regulations in the preparation of  ABC
    for its future projects;


 b. Municipal Accountant demand payment or institute appropriate action for
    the collection of the excess contract cost  totaling  ₱339,990.18  from
    the concerned contractors, as the case may be; and

 c. Municipal Engineer, BAC and concerned employees adhere with the  above-
    mentioned laws, rules and regulations and in their future projects.

a. Municipal Engineer (i) monitor and  require  the  contractors  to  make
corrective actions on the defects noted in the Local Access Road,  Brgy
B. del Mundo and Rehabilitation and Upgrading of Matagbak- Teresita  to
Panaytayan All Weather Road, Phase III and apprise the  Audit  Team  on
the actions taken thereto; (ii) provide acceptable justification on the
observations relative to the contract cost for the  Rehabilitation  and
Upgrading of Matagbak- Teresita to Panaytayan All Weather  Road,  Phase
III; (iii) submit the  lacking  documents  for  the  two  projects  for
further review  and  evaluation  of  the  COA  TAS;  and  (iii)  ensure
compliance with COA Resolution No. 2015-014 dated  April  6,  2015  and
DPWH DO No. 197 dated October 7,  2016 and other applicable laws,  rules
and regulations in the preparation of ABC for its future projects;


 b. Municipal Accountant demand payment or institute appropriate action for
the collection of the excess contract cost totaling ₱35,169.62 from the
concerned contractors, as the case may be; and

 c. Municipal Engineer, BAC and concerned  employees  provide  satisfactory
explanation on  the  excess  quantity  of  Item  No.  311  (PCCP)  with
reference to the approved POW for the Local Access Road, Brgy.  B.  del
Mundo, Mansalay, Oriental Mindoro.

We recommended that the:

   a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal  Planning
      Development Coordinator (i) plan the procurement and implementation of
      the identified priority development  projects  under  the  20%  DF  to
      ensure  the  completion  of  the  PPAs;  (ii)  coordinate   with   the
      beneficiaries/concerned offices with PPAs that will be included in the
      20% DF AIP to determine the specific works to be done in each PPAs and
      to  avoid  delays  in  the  implementation;  and  (iii)  evaluate  the
      feasibility of the particular PPAs and  observe  timeliness  in  their
      implementation to achieve the desired socio-economic  development  and
      environmental outcomes;


   b. Municipal Engineer submit the list of completed projects  to  the  MBO
      and  MAccO  to  facilitate  the  reversion  and  re-appropriation   of
      savings/unexpended balances from completed projects under the  20%  DF
      to  eligible   development   projects,   and   follow   strictly   the
      implementation period as indicated in the AIP/PPMP; and


   c. Municipal Engineer and the BAC provide periodically the  MBO  and  the
      MAccO with the status of projects and the procurements  undertaken  by
      the Municipality to assist  the  concerned  offices  in  the  periodic
      review and monitoring  of the  balances  of  current  and  continuing
      appropriations and inform immediately the MBO and MAccO whenever there
      are changes  on  the  awarded  PPAs  and  problems  in  the  project’s
      implementation to make  the  necessary  adjustment  in  the  financial
      reports relative to the affected PPAs.

We recommended that the:

 a. MDRRM Officer monitor and facilitate the  implementation  of  the  PPAs
    under the LDRRMF by monitoring the period  of  its  implementation  and
    ensure that all projects appropriated in LDRRMF be  implemented  during
    the  year;  and   optimize  the  utilization  of  the  LDRRMF  of   the
    Municipality  to  strengthen  its  disaster  preparedness   and   avoid
    potential damages during disasters; and


 b. MPDC, MDRRMC and MRRM Officer provide specific, measurable, achievable,
    realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators for  each  PPAs
    appropriated in the MDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the:

   a. Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the enactment of a Local  Solid  Waste
      Management Ordinance, among others, to operationalize requirements  of
      the RA No. 9003 and facilitate effective implementation of SWM agendas
      of the Municipality;


   b. MSWMB (i) immediately address the requirements of the  NSWMC  for  the
      approval of the Municipality’s 10-year SWMP; (ii) assess the  manpower
      requirements in the implementation of the  SWM  of  the  Municipality;
      (iii) enforce close supervision with the BSWMC to ensure that they are
      all active and functional;  (iv)  promote  continuous  segregation  of
      waste at source through IEC drives and assess  regularly  the  funding
      and resource requirements of the  SWM  to  ensure  implementation  and
      realization of all SWM programs and projects; and (v) through the Liga
      ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay  officials
      to support the SWM  program  of  the  government  by  educating  their
      respective constituents on  the  proper  segregation  of  reusable  or
      recyclable  materials  from  compostable  wastes  and  maintenance  of
      materials recovery facilities; and

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman, Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials ensure compliance with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 including timely posting of NOA in PhilGEPS, proper execution of contract documents, imposition of liquidated damages where applicable, submission of all lacking documents and issuance of Certificate of Availability of Funds as required under Section 86 of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer provide (i) specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-bound (SMART) performance indicators in each PPAs appropriated for MDRRM Fund to easily quantify the attainment of the planned PPAs; (ii) specific period of implementation of the PPAs of MDRRM; (iii) avoid inclusion of administrative expenses in the LDRRMFIP and see to it that only PPAs under the four (4) thematic areas were prioritized in the 70% MF; Municipal Accountant (i) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statement the ongoing PPAs or projects recorded under Construction in Progress accounts utilized out of LDRRMF-Special Trust Fund for reconciliation of the balance of Trust Liability DRRMF account in the financial statement and the unutilized amount reported in the RSULDRRMF; and (ii) provide acceptable justification on the improper charging of various PPAs that lead to its non-implementation; and MDRRMO, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman fast track the implementation of the PPAs under the MDRRM Fund by monitoring the period for which the PPAs will be implemented. Henceforth, ensure that all projects appropriated in MDRRM Fund be implemented during the year it was appropriated in order to increase the capacity of the Municipality to cope with potential hazards and disasters.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant, MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer committed to abide with recommendation of the Audit Team. MDRRMO, Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman to fast track implementation.
observations.reason: The concerned employee is loaded with heavy responsibilities thus pressure of equally important matters and sufficient time to comply with Audit Team’s recommendati on.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Human Resource Management Officer: (a) submit justification on the necessity of hiring of 236 contract of service personnel for CY 2018; (b) formulate hiring plan of job orders and contract of service to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto taking into consideration the provisions of CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 dated June 15, 2017; (c) conduct a thorough study and evaluation of the Municipality’s needs for additional personnel to avoid excessive hiring of individuals under contract of services and/or job orders and the duplication of the work being undertaken by regular employees; and (d) as a control measure, ensure that all the job order contracts entered into by the Municipality clearly indicate the specific work or task to be assigned to each job order to facilitate comparison with the actual and accomplishment report be duly signed and verified by the immediate supervisor to prevent from unnecessary expenditures.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:


   a. Municipal Accountant and  Municipal  Treasurer  reconcile  their  cash
      records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts  per
      GL and cashbooks;


   b. Municipal Treasurer continue to make representation and follow-up with
      the (i) depository bank and request copies of  the  credit  and  debit
      memoranda totalling P385,092.06 and P1,279,326.21,  respectively,  and
      provide a copy  of  which  to  the  Municipal  Accountant  for  proper
      adjustment, if warranted;  and  (ii)  Rural  Bank  of  Pola  and  LBP-
      Pinamalayan regarding the deposits totalling  P675,618.32  which  were
      still not credited in the Municipality’s LBP-Pinamalayan and LBP-Roxas
      account; and


   c. Municipal Accountant (i) submit the BRS  for  the  13  remaining  bank
      accounts for the month of December 2019;  (ii)  ensure  the  immediate
      preparation of all BRS for all bank accounts; (iii)  conduct  detailed
      review and checking  of  bank  records/reports  and  request  for  the
      necessary documents to support the recording of the reconciling items,
      if any; (iv) exert effort to substantiate the  identified  reconciling
      items and  prepare  the  necessary  adjustments;  (v)  reclassify  the
      interest income earned and received totalling P112,938.49 to  cash  in
      bank account, if warranted, and stop the practice of waiting  for  the
      collection voucher of the Municipal  Treasurer  before  recording  the
      said income  in  the  accounting  books;  and  (vi)  review  the  long
      outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks without  identified
      payees, and require the Municipal Treasurer to  cancel  the  unclaimed
      stale checks and furnish the Accountant with the original DVs and  its
      supporting documents for the preparation  of  the  JEV  following  the
      procedures prescribed under the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to  ensure
      recording of such cancellation.

   financial assistance to LGUs  recorded  as  expense  to  the  appropriate

   submit liquidation report in accordance with COA  Circular  No.  2015-009
   dated December 1, 2015 and COA Circular No.  94-013  dated  December  13,
   1994.

We recommended that the:

      Accountant; and


   b. Municipal Accountant (i) demand for the payment of liquidated  damages
      as computed by the Municipal Engineer, and the excess contract cost of
      P634,211.69 as computed by the COA TAS; and (ii)  submit  the  lacking
      documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile their cash
records and settle the disparity in the balances of cash accounts per GL and
cashbooks;
b. Municipal Treasurer (i) ensure that collections be deposited in proper
account; (ii) prepare disbursement voucher to transfer the collections of
General Fund erroneously deposited in Trust Fund accounts; (iii) make
representation with the depository bank and request for a copy of the credit
and debit memoranda totalling P518,474.04 and P1,700,656.34, respectively,
and provide a copy of which to the Municipal Accountant for proper
adjustment, if warranted; and (iv) make representation with the Rural
Bank of Pola and LBP-Pinamalayan regarding the deposits totalling
P1,109,478.34 which were still not credited in the Municipality’s LBP-
Pinamalayan and LBP-Roxas account; and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) ensure immediate preparation of all bank
reconciliation statements for all bank accounts; (ii) conduct detailed review
and checking of bank records/reports and request for the necessary
documents to support the recording of the reconciling items, if any; (iii)
exert effort to substantiate the identified reconciling items and after which
prepare the necessary adjustments; and (iv) review the long
outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks without identified payees,
and require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks
and furnish the Accountant with the original DVs and its supporting
documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures
prescribed under the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to ensure recording of
such cancellation.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipal Mayor facilitate the submission of the cash advance
voucher, liquidation report and supporting documents pertaining to the
Intelligence Fund amounting to P837,825.00 granted to him to COA-
ICFAO for audit purposes;
b. Municipal Accountant require the officers and employees to liquidate
cash advances within the prescribed period, ensure strict compliance with
Sections 4.1.7 and 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10,
1997; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant seek the assistance of the
Municipal Civil Registrar and Punong Barangays to locate those
individuals with outstanding balances from the livelihood loans granted
by MGM, and prepare and send demand letters to remind them of their
obligations.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer coordinate with each
other and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper
reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of the actual
existence, ownership, and condition of the properties;
b. Municipal Accountant analyze the nature of transactions and make an
adjustment to properly classify the items to a more appropriate account,
and effect the transfer of PPE in Trust Fund to General Fund in
compliance with Sections 96 and 104 of the Manual on the NGAS for
LGU;
c. Municipal Treasurer: (i) prepare and maintain appropriate property
records and reports for PPE account, (ii) account all determined
unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of IIRUP and other
required supporting documents for disposal, and (iii) prepare and
maintain RLRN and Road Map to account for the Local Roads and
reconcile with the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the
Accounting Office; and (iv) make appropriate action in order that the
procured ambulance be in compliant with the provision of DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101 dated September 23, 2010.

Henceforth, ensure that all vehicles to be procured by MGM be compliant
with the said Memorandum Circular;
d. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count and technical
valuation of the local road network to determine the current value of the
road components to facilitate the preparation of the RPCLRN; and
e. Municipal Engineer maintain road map and provide the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with complete description and
cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to
reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds;
and review and analyze the causes of the discrepancies and take up necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts. Likewise, ensure compliance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 on the use of
appropriate account for every transaction.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) ensure that only
obligations with approved disbursement voucher and complete supporting
documents, with delivery/services rendered, be booked as accounts payable
pursuant to the prevailing rules and regulation; and (b) review the schedule of
accounts payable and reconcile records with creditors in order to ascertain if there
is still a valid claim, if none, accounts payable outstanding for more than two years
should be reverted to the unappropriated surplus, and provide this Office of the
result of such review.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant identify the long outstanding difference and prepare the necessary
adjustment to establish the correct balance of the Loans Payable account; and
provide satisfactory explanation on the payment of the loan amortization even
without valid appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require the contractors to
make corrective actions on the defects noted in the Rehabilitation with Road
Opening and Spot Concreting of Sta. Brigida to Alegria Farm-to-Market Road
(FMR) and Concreting of Balugo to Maliwanag Farm-to-Market Road (FMR), or
demand for payment of the equivalent cost of P122,175.15. Moreover, submit the
needed documents for the proper evaluation and computation of liquidated
damages and negative slippage in accordance with Annex “E” of Section 8 of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184 and DPWH Department Order No. 102 dated November 8,
1988.

We recommended that the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (i) ensure that the bidding documents
were properly dated in order to verify the validity and propriety of each of
the documents submitted by the prospective bidder; (ii) post the NOA in
PhilGEPS website, in agency’s website, and three conspicuous places as
required under Sections 37.1.6 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and (iii)
provide acceptable justification on other noted observations regarding the
procurement of the projects;
b. Municipal Engineer: (i) compute the amount of liquidated damages
pursuant to Item 8(1) of Annex “E” of IRR-A of RA No. 9184 caused by
the delays incurred in the implementation of Construction/Opening of Sta.
Brigida – Alegria FMR, and demand from the contractor the amount due.
Henceforth, ensure compliance with the imposition of liquidated damages
for delayed projects; (ii) provide justification in the inconsistencies noted
in the implementation of its projects; (iii) provide explanation on the
granting of invalid reasons for suspension of work pursuant to Item 9.2 of
Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and (iv) provide justification on
other noted observations regarding the implementation of the projects;
and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) provide justification on the non-issuance of
Certificate of Availability of Funds, and ensure compliance with Section
86 of PD 1445 regarding the posting of certificate of availability of funds,
and (ii) submit all the lacking documents noted for audit purposes.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Chairman provide acceptable justification on (i) execution of Affidavit
of Site Inspection of the winning bidder for the Construction of
Multipurpose Building prior to the posting of the Invitation to Bid in
PhilGEPS, (ii) delay on the posting of NOA in PhilGEPS, (iii) delay in the
execution of performance security and contract agreement; (iv) noted
observation on the date of execution of Performance Bond, Contractor’s All
Risk Insurance and Warranty Security, (v) delay in the posting of NOA in
PhilGEPS; and (vi) all other observations noted in the procurement of the
projects;
b. Municipal Engineer compute the delays incurred by the contractor in the
construction of multi-purpose disregarding the invalid issued suspension
order, and demand the contractor to pay the liquidated damages; and
c. Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking documents for audit purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the
Municipality by educating their respective constituents on the proper
segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;
b. through the MENRO-Designate, (i) take appropriate action to comply with
the requirements of NSWMC regarding the conditional approval of the
Municipality’s 10-year solid waste management plan; (ii) comply with the
requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with
proper labels on the designated garbage collection area and other public
places and collecting only those wastes which are properly segregated; and
(iii) establish MRF in the dumpsite area;
c. recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures
that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No.
9003; and
d. see to it that the operation of the open dumpsite is properly controlled and in
compliance with Rule XIII- Operation of Controlled Dumpsite of the IRR of
RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the (a) Local School Board prepare the PPMP for all
the procurement to be undertaken in the SEF and submit the same to the BAC

Maria to Villarosa Farm-to-Market Road to Marcphil Construction, Construction and Orient Star Construction Inc., respectively, even performance security was not posted; (iii) entering into a contract agreement with the contractors prior to issuance of Notice of Award; (iv) non-posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed in PhilGEPS website, agency website, if any, and any conspicuous places; (v) release of retention money even the contractor was beyond schedule; (vi) non-attachment of complete supporting documents in the paid disbursement vouchers violating Section 4(6) of PD 1445; (vii) compute the amount of liquidated damages and demand for payment from the contractor; and (b) Municipal Engineer submit acceptable justification on the other observations noted in the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: BAC Chairman submitted explanation letter dated September 10, 2018 to COA on November 12, 2018. Municipal Engineer submitted explanation letter dated April 4, 2018 to COA on April 13, 2018 and a follow-up explanation dated October 15, 2018 submitted on November 6, 2018.
observations.reason: Infractions on procurement provisions; Submission of acceptable justifications of concerned employees are still in process. Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant submit acceptable justification on (i) the issuance of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer of the Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) for the procurement of materials for the Rehabilitation of Wasig Irrigation System and Drainage Canal at Brgy. Roma totaling P996,520.90, instead of her pursuant to Section 86 of PD 1445; (ii) the non-imposition of warranty security to the contractor pursuant to Section 62.1 of the IRR of RA No. 9184; and (iii) incorrect accounting entries made on the implementation of the projects; (b) BAC Chairman submit acceptable justification on the posting of Performance Security of GMG Lumber and Hardware for the Rehabilitation of Wasig Irrigation System prior to receipt of the Notice of Award; (c) Municipal Engineer submit satisfactory justification on the (i) excess of actual men-days versus the programmed men-days in POW in the Rehabilitation of Wasig Irrigation System; and (ii) delayed implementation of the projects; (d) Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman, and Municipal Engineer submit (i) acceptable justification on the other observations noted in the procurement and implementation of the projects; and (ii) all the lacking documents for audit purpose; and (e) Municipal Mayor submit acceptable justification on returning the P0.50 million allocated by Department of Agriculture for cassava plantation in the Municipality, thus, resulting to its non-implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted justification and will submit the Revised Certificate of Availability of Funds. Verified JEV and found out that the entry made on JEV was incorrect but was posted correctly in the Ledgers.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant submit acceptable justification (i) on the issuance of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer of the Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) for the contract of detailed engineering design, civil works and expenditures charged against the Engineering and Administrative Overheads amounting to P996,520.90, instead of her pursuant to Section 86 of PD 1445; (ii) the non-imposition of warranty security to the contractor pursuant to Section 62.1 of the IRR of RA No. 9184; and (iii) incorrect accounting entries made on the implementation of the projects; (b) BAC Chairman submit acceptable justification on the posting of Performance Security of GMG Lumber and Hardware for the Rehabilitation of Wasig Irrigation System prior to receipt of the Notice of Award; (c) Municipal Engineer submit satisfactory justification on the (i) excess of actual men-days versus the programmed men-days in POW in the Rehabilitation of Wasig Irrigation System; and (ii) delayed implementation of the projects; (d) Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman, and Municipal Engineer submit (i) acceptable justification on the other observations noted in the procurement and implementation of the projects; and (ii) all the lacking documents for audit purpose; and (e) Municipal Mayor submit acceptable justification on returning the P0.50 million allocated by Department of Agriculture for cassava plantation in the Municipality, thus, resulting to its non-implementation.

observations.reason: Submission of acceptable justifications of other concerned employees are still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant submit acceptable justification (i) on the issuance of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer of the Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) for the contract of detailed engineering design, civil works and expenditures charged against Engineering and Administrative Overheads (ESAO) for the Construction/Expansion of Water Supply System Level II – Phase III at Barangay Panaytayan, Mansalay totalling P10,046,037.28; (ii) non-imposition of warranty security to the contractors; (iii) incorrect accounting entries made on the implementation of the projects; (b) BAC Chairman submit acceptable justification on the issuance of Notice of Award even there is no available cash contrary to Item 5.7.2 of DBM CL No. 2010-9; and (c) Municipal Engineer submit acceptable justification on the issuance of suspension orders during the implementation of the project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted justification and revised the Certificate of Availability of Funds for the contract of detailed engineering design, civil works and expenditures charged against the ES A O. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant submit acceptable justification (i) on the issuance of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer of the Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) for the procurement of materials for fish canning production amounting to P926,640.00, instead of her pursuant to Section 86 of PD 1445; (ii) the non-imposition of warranty security to the contractor pursuant to Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (iii) compute liquidated damages (LD) for the items not yet delivered totaling P291,600.00 for every day of delay until fully delivered and require the supplier to pay the corresponding LD, to avoid disallowance in audit; (b) Municipal Mayor and the Inspection Officer submit acceptable justification on certifying the completeness and compliance with specification of the delivered items for the fish canning production amounting to P925,440.00 even the supplier failed to deliver the complete items and were not in accordance with the specifications contrary to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012, to avoid disallowance in audit; (c) BAC Chairman submit acceptable justification on the observations noted in the procurement of various supplies and materials in the production of fish canning; and (d) Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman submit all the lacking documents, to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submitted acceptable justification on the charging of the fish canning production to BFAR and submitted the revised CAF. Municipal Mayor and Inspection Officer submitted explanation letter dated September 10, 2018 to COA on November 16, 2018.
observations.reason: Lacking documents are not yet submitted since the Municipality still has to finish review of all documents to determine which is lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) BAC Chairman submit acceptable justification on the splitting of contracts for the procurement of display cabinet, stainless table and cooling racks totaling P130,000.00; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) submit acceptable justification on the non-issuance of Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) on the procurement relative the implementation of sweet kaong production; (ii) compute the liquidated damages for the 4.5 sacks of kaong still not delivered totaling P11,700.00 for every day of delay until fully delivered and require the supplier to pay the corresponding LD; (iii) increase in unit price of the kaong even there is only one PO issued for the procurement; and (c) PESO Coordinator submit acceptable justification on the acceptance in staggered basis of the kaong. Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor ensure that all the budgeted items needed in the production be implemented to help the beneficiary organization and fully realized the objective of the program, and details in the Purchase Order are completely filled up pursuant to Section 74 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property management in the Local Governments.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submitted relative justification on the implementation of sweet kaong production, and a revised CAF was submitted. Municipal Mayor and BAC Chairman submitted explanation letter dated September 10, 2018 to COA on November 16, 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant submit acceptable justification on the (i) issuance of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer of the Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) for the procurement of bulldozer and heavy equipment totaling P8.00 million, instead of her pursuant to Section 86 of PD 1445; and (ii) the non-imposition of warranty security to the contractor pursuant to Section 62.1 of IRR of RA No. 9184; (b) Municipal Treasurer submit acceptable justification on the payment of the bulldozer even there was still no delivery made by the supplier contrary to Section 88(1) of PD 1445; (c) BAC Chairman submit acceptable justification on declaring SRV 888 International Trading Corporation eligible for the procurement of road grader despite with insufficient Net Financial Contracting Capacity as per Section 23.4.2.6 of IRR of RA No. 9184; and (d) Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman submit (i) acceptable justification on the other observations noted in the procurement of bulldozer and road grader; and (ii) all the lacking documents for audit purpose.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant submitted acceptable justification and revised the CAF. Likewise, BAC Chairman/Budget Officer submitted an explanation letter dated September 10, 2018 to COA on November 12, 2018 along with lacking documents needed.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) End-User Office or Implementing Units submit their PPMPs to the Municipal Budget Office for checking and not to Treasurer’s Office; (b) Municipal Budget Officer after checking the consistency of the submitted PPMPs forward the same to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation and preparation of APP; (c) BAC Chairman abide with the provision of Section 12.1 of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 by implementing only the staff of the BAC Office in the conduct of procurement activities of the Municipality; (d) BAC and GSO consider the procurement of common-use supplies in the Procurement Service of the Department of Budget and Management (PS-DBM) or bulk purchases to avail of volume discount and lesser paper work and travelling cost; (e) BAC Chairman submit acceptable justification on the acceptance of quotes above the Purchase Request/Approved Budget for the Contract amount in relation to Section 52.1(b) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and (f) Municipal Accountant provide explanation on the attachment of undated Sales Invoice/Official Receipts as supporting documents for the procurement of commonly used supplies and equipment.
observations.managementAction: End-user officers already submitted their PPMPs to the Budget Office while the MBO checked the consistency of the submitted PPMPs then forwarded to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation and preparation of APP.
observations.reason: No available storage room in the Municipal Compound where they can store their inventories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal officials and employees refund the excessive claims for travelling expenses totalling P81,515.82. Henceforth, comply with the provision of Section 4 of EO No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 in preparing the claim for travelling expenses and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Concerned municipal employees partially refunded the excessive payment of per diem. Henceforth, comply with the provision of Section 4 of EO No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 in preparing the claim for travelling expenses and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for the complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Due to lack of available funds of the concerned employees, thus refund could not be facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor submit acceptable justification on the granting of mobile phone units to eighteen (18) municipal employees and contract of service workers which are considered lower than a Division Chief level, otherwise the transaction shall be disallowed.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor submitted an explanation letter dated August 21, 2018 to COA on November 16, 2018.
observations.reason: No further action was taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) MSWDO prepare and come up with well-defined guidelines and policies for the granting of financial assistance to indigent constituents; (b) Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer explain why the disbursement of AICS exceeds the budgeted amount for CY 2017; (c) Municipal Accountant ensure that all supporting documents are correct and complete; and (d) Municipal Mayor and MSWDO submit acceptable justification on the observations noted in the granting of AICS.
observations.managementAction: End-user officers already implemented AICS to indigent individuals in accordance with Section 3.1(c) of DSWD Administrative Order No. 01, Series of 2011. Municipal Accountant ensures that all supporting documents are correct and complete.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous workload detailed analysis of appropriation vis-à-vis the obligations could not be facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal School Board and Municipal Budget Officer follow the provision of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. of 2017 dated January 19, 2017 in preparing the SEF budget; and (b) Municipal School Board, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant (i) review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160; (ii) strictly follow the regulation on the procurement of goods; and (iii) submit the missing documents and acceptable justification on the observations noted in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: Municipal School Board, MBO and Municipal Accountant reviewed and analyzed the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus. Lacking documents were already submitted.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower and voluminous workload to consolidate procurement documents could not be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the MENRO-Designate, (i) take appropriate action to comply with the requirements of NSWMC regarding the conditional approval of the Municipality’s 10-year solid waste management plan; (ii) comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels on the designated garbage collection area and other public places and collecting only those wastes which are properly segregated; and (iii) establish MRF in the dumpsite area; (c) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003; and (d) see to it that the operation of the open dumpsite is properly controlled and in compliance with Rule XIII- Operation of Controlled Dumpsite of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Mayor recommended to the SB for the passage of Anti-Littering Policy Ordinance. The Municipality provided additional trash bins with labels. Cascading of utilization of MRF in the barangays was in process.
observations.reason: The process of procuring additional trash bins with labels was in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Budget Officer allocate the required 1% of their IRA for LCPC programs/projects/activities as mandated by Section 15 of RA 9344; and (b) MSWDO abide with the rules and regulations with regards to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund as provided by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer allocated the required 1% of the municipality’s IRA for LCPC programs/projects/activities as mandated by Section 15 of RA 9344. Municipality already conformed with the rules and regulations in the formulation of budget and utilization of 1% LCPC fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Budget Officer and MSWDO ensures that programs/projects/activities that address the concerns of senior citizens and persons with disability be included in the Annual Budget of the Municipality, if possible appropriate at least 1% of the total appropriation pursuant to DILG MC No. 2014-59 dated May 9, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Budget Officer and MSWDO ensured that PPAs addressed the concerns of senior citizens and PWDs and that corresponding appropriations have been included in the Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer’s Offices immediately submit the original copy of the 14 paid disbursement vouchers amounting to P1,464,104.69 together with all the supporting documents to COA Office for verification and audit purposes. Henceforth, the Municipal Accounting and the Treasurer’s Offices adhere strictly to the provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 relative the submission of reports and accounts to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the DVs to COA on May 18, 2018.
observations.reason: Due to lack of available personnel collating of documents could not be facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that:(a) In the planning stage, the Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (i) consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and the climate or weather factor in stating the period of implementation of the programs/projects/activities; and (ii) evaluate the viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year; (b) Municipal Engineer and the concerned implementing department heads: (i) periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the MGM as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, (ii) submit a list of all completed projects to support the reprogramming to other developmental projects or reversion to the unappropriated surplus; and (iii) strictly follow the planned implementation period as per the duly approved Annual Investment Plan; and (c) Local Development Council, Municipal Budget Officer and other concerned officials refrain from charging lump-sum appropriations under the 20% Development Funds and be guided by the proposed priority developmental projects as provided by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The LDC, Implementing Office and MPDC already considered urgency and weather factors in the implementation of PPAs and evaluated the viability of the projects as well as the need for additional funding. MBO and Municipal Accountant reviewed and analyzed the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus. LDC Secretariat headed by MPDC submitted to COA on the charging of lumpsum appropriations.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to fully implement the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (i) require the supplier to immediately deliver the lacking materials, for the Sulong Manggagawa OFWs to commence on the production of the livelihood program; (ii) identify the person who will be accountable for the waste of government funds due to expiration of olive oil and rusting of tin cans, and make that person liable; and (iii) MPDC and other concerned office extend help to Sulong Manggagawa OFWs of Mansalay in order to realize the objective of the program.
observations.managementAction: Funds used to construct the Balugo Public Market came from Kalahi-CIDSS NCDDP, and the municipal counterpart. Kalahi National Project Monitoring office required the beneficiaries to submit an Operation and Maintenance Plan for the project but the plan was not implemented due to limited resources. MPDC and concerned offices extended help to Sulong Manggagawa OFWs of Mansalay to realize the objective of the program. Expired items and unsuitable equipment were already replaced by the supplier.
observations.reason: Expired items and unsuituble equipment were already replaced by the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the barangay officials require the non-profit organization/people’s organization that implemented the project to maintain this building to avoid illegal use and further deterioration of the building. We further recommend that the Mayor and the barangay officials require the non-profit organization/people’s organization that implemented the project to prepare a concrete plan and strategy to attract vendors to occupy the space and negotiate with them the construction of their own stall or offer the space for rent to private organization to avoid further deterioration of the building. We further recommended that the Barangay require the tenants to maintain the building and provide additional funding to secure the project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality provided additional fund to secure the project. The Balugo Public Market came from Kalahi-CIDSS NCDDP and the municipal counterpart. Kalahi Project Monitoring Office required the beneficiaries to submit an Operation and Maintenance Plan for the project. The municipality provided additional funding to secure the project.
observations.reason: Strategy to attract tenants was not yet formulated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the:(a) Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to work hand-in-hand to update their records especially those properties with multiple owners, which could include actual field verification of the properties and appropriate updating of records; (b) Municipal Treasurer devise various strategies and activities to facilitate effective revenue collection by: (i) comprehensive tax information campaign; and (ii) integration of tax mapping process into the iTAX system. Moreover, implement remedy provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160 and explore all possible remedies as provided under RA No.7160 and relevant regulations to collect those delinquent accounts for more than five years; and (c) Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor exert extra efforts to reconcile their records pertaining to real property taxes, effect the necessary adjustments, if any and ensure consistent adjustment of real property tax receivable upon collection.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Assessor updated their records and conducted house-to-house campaign. Municipal Treasurer distributed notices to delinquent property owners.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and voluminous workload that are equally important.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Municipal Administrator, Agriculturist and HRMO submit acceptable justification on the (i) non-issuance of job-order appointment to job-order personnel hired for the implementation of Greening and Reforestation Program, Marine Protected Area; and Municipal-Wide Clean-Up Drive Program; and (ii) accomplishment reports that were pre-printed and general statement of accomplishment of the hired job-order personnel; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) identify the persons responsible for the excess payment of wages totaling P115,150.00, and demand for payment to avoid disallowance in audit; and (ii) submit all the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit. Hereafter, ensure documents that will support the veracity, propriety and regularity of the claim be attached, before payment.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Administrator, Agriculturist and HRMO submitted acceptable justification letters.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to collate the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Human Resource Management Officer: (a) Conduct a thorough study and evaluation of the Municipality’s needs for additional personnel to avoid excessive hiring of individuals under contract of services and/or job orders and the duplication of the work being undertaken by regular employees; (b) As a control measure, ensure that all the job order contracts entered into by the Municipality clearly indicate the specific work or task to be assigned to each job order to facilitate comparison with the actual and accomplishment report be duly signed and verified by the immediate supervisor to prevent from unnecessary expenditures; (c) Formulate hiring plan of contract of service to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto; and (d) Submit acceptable justification on the noted observations pertaining the hiring of 246 contract of services for CY 2017.
observations.managementAction: The specific work or tasked to be assigned to personnel were clearly indicated in their job description forms filed in their 201 files. Drafting of hiring plan and thorough study and evaluation of the needs for additional personnel to avoid excessive hiring of individuals under COS or JOs is ongoing.
observations.reason: Drafting of hiring plan and thorough study and evaluation of the needs for additional personnel to avoid excessive hiring of individuals under COS or JOs is ongoing.

observations.reason: Lack of manpower to prepare and submit GPB within the set deadline.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: (a) ensure that collections
of the collectors are within the maximum cash accountability allowed in their
fidelity bond, if possible coordinate with the Bureau of Treasury for the increase in
their fidelity bonds; (b) refrain from issuing accountable forms to non-bonded
employees pursuant to Section 101 of PD 1445, Section 305(f) of RA No.7160,
Section 50, Chapter 9, Subtitle B, Book V, EO No 292 and Bureau of Treasury,
Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 5, 2009. Likewise, ensure that non-
bonded employees are properly supervise by the bonded employee and install
control measures in the collection of the non-bonded employees; (c) discontinue the
practice of keeping large amount of collections in her hand/possession to avoid
possible loss and misappropriation. Likewise, deposit the collections intact every
day or as regular as possible; (d) require the Liquidating Officer to verify whether
the collections pertaining the used and all issued accountable forms are remitted
before new series of forms are issued to the collectors; and (e) submit
comments/justification on the above noted observation in the collection and deposit,
and issuance and use of accountable forms.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) submit acceptable
justification on the discrepancies amounting to P6,911.26 noted in the unadjusted
balance per book appearing in the trial balance and bank reconciliation statements
as of December 31, 2017; (ii) submit the bank reconciliation statements for the
months of January to October 2017 for the High Yield Savings Account of the
Municipality; and (iii) prepare the adjusting entries to revert to the proper cash and
payable accounts pertaining to stale checks totaling P137,382.58 at year-end; (b)
Municipal Treasurer (i) make representation with the depository bank and request
for a copy of the credit and debit memo totalling P1,538,272.19 and P821,490.94,
respectively, and provide a copy of which to the Municipal Accountant for proper
adjustment, if warranted; (ii) make representation with Rural Bank of Pola and

We recommended that: (a) The Municipal Mayor directs the Inventory Team to conduct complete inventory of all of its property, plant and equipment. Proper planning should be considered such as preparation of plan specifying the date and timeframe of physical inventory, and the composition of the team who will conduct the physical inventory; previous inventory reports and the list of PPEs per type/category from the accounting office must be available for ready reference; and ARE must be available for fast tracing of the property and equipment. During the conduct of inventory, the Committee should list all PPEs found on hand. Property tagging must be made. Semi-expendable items should be identified and segregated from the inventory report of PPEs and must be prepared in a separate report. After the physical inventory-taking, the inventory committee shall reconcile the results of the count with property and accounting records; (b) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain ledger and stock or property cards, respectively, and undertake the reconciliation of the result of the physical count of PPE with their respective records for fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements. Moreover, the MT may begin to maintain property records based on the deliveries of supplies or property for CY 2016 to date on the basis of the AIR and other supporting documents; and (c) The Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officers to undertake the necessary actions regarding the disposal of its unserviceable property, plant and equipment in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and Section 194 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments. Moreover, assess the utility of the fully depreciated assets and consider disposing, in accordance with relevant regulations, those of no use to prevent its further deterioration.
observations.managementAction: Complete inventory of all PPE was not yet conducted while the RPCPPE submitted on February 2, 2018 was incomplete and not fully accomplished. Ledger cards were not maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Property and ledger cards on were still not maintained due to the limited number of the Accounting and GSO personnel. Thus, reconciliati on of the result of the physical count was not facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: (a) The Municipal Accountant to analyze the recorded Road Network then prepare a subsidiary record for each component of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card; (b) The Municipal Treasurer to maintain Local Road Inventory Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card. Moreover, conduct physical count of the road network and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) and submit the same to this office not later than January 31 of each year; and (c) The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer already forwarded partial list of complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the Municipal Treasurer, however, the latter was not able to conduct physical inventory of the list of road network provided, thus the RPCRN was not maintained.

observations.managementAction: Cash advances of the Special Disbursing Officer, Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers totaling P696,900.00 were liquidated.
observations.reason: Cash advance of Armando Samarita, former Municipal Vice Mayor amounting to P39,550.00 remained unliquidated. Demand letter prepared by the Municipal Accountant was not accepted Mr. Samarita

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant exhaust all remedies provided under existing rules and regulations and the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply and submit the necessary documents to the ICFAO to effect the settlement of the unliquidated Intelligence/Confidential Fund; (b) Municipal Treasurer prohibits the SDO to transfer its CA to another Accountable Officer and strictly comply to Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002; (c) Municipal Accountant and concerned municipal employees to submit the Pre and Post Inspection Report the above mentioned transactions as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; (d) BAC Chairman revisit RA 9184 and its IRR in order to apply the correct method of procurement for every government transactions; and (e) SDO liquidate its CAs as soon as the purpose for which it has been given is served as prescribed under Section 5.1.2 of CAO Circular No. 97-002 and Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004.
observations.reason: Management still granted the SDO and GSO-Designated cash advances for the purchase of equipment and other supplies.
